what_good_looks_like: One command creates a repo-specific constitution, AGENTS.md, REVIEW.md, docs contract, validation commands, PR template, and evidence receipt format.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit has install profiles/templates, opt-in Node/Python stack-hygiene profiles, Renovate-style onboarding instructions, profile/config metadata migration markers, explicit metadata-only migration commands, and a managed guided upgrade flow for installed repos. AGW-100 made repo-contract-kit the public operator surface, but AGW-127 is needed to turn the remaining two-repo source/install history into one version 1 repository and release train.
external_examples: github/spec-kit; BMAD-METHOD; Renovate onboarding PR; AGENTS.md
ideas_to_borrow: Use phased bootstrap: principles, specs, plan, tasks, checks. Finish the v1 consolidation so the public curl installer, target enrollment, workflow source, release metadata, and archive redirects all live under one repository identity.
gap_or_risk: The operator surface is simpler
priority: but source ownership and release identity still carry historical two-repo seams until the v1 consolidation is planned and migrated.

what_good_looks_like: Short root instructions route to scoped files for docs, review, tests, design, security, and runtime with clear precedence and nested overrides.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit has prompt files. repo-contract-kit templates AGENTS.md/REVIEW.md, managed runtime adapters, and opt-in private-context .agent-context examples, but does not yet emit a full instruction index across agent runtimes. The legacy source checkout also needs its route maps refreshed after AGW-100 so agents do not land new workflow-source edits in the archive repo.
external_examples: AGENTS.md; Claude Code CLAUDE.md; Cursor .cursor/rules; Windsurf .windsurf/rules; Devin REVIEW.md; Stitch DESIGN.md
ideas_to_borrow: Generate AGENTS.md, REVIEW.md, DESIGN.md optional contract, .cursor/rules, .continue/rules, and compatibility notes from one canonical policy.
gap_or_risk: Instruction drift and duplicated guidance across tools can make agents follow stale or conflicting rules; local context can leak if it escapes the opt-in/redaction boundary.

what_good_looks_like: CI checks prove referenced paths and commands exist, rules are concise, no hidden Unicode/secrets exist, permissions are narrow, and tool-specific files do not conflict.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit now checks hidden Unicode, secrets, wildcard permissions, path/command freshness, contradictions, bloat/provenance, instruction budgets, and no-write instruction-diet offload recommendations for route-map drift, duplicated procedure, and stale/localizable command detail.
external_examples: agents-lint; aidevshield; microsoft/agent-governance-toolkit; HN AGENTS.md discussion
ideas_to_borrow: Keep calibrating warning-to-error thresholds and runtime-adapter compatibility while preserving reviewable proposal flows before automatic pruning.
gap_or_risk: Agent instructions remain a supply-chain and correctness surface; strictness still needs repo-specific calibration so useful route-map context is not pruned too aggressively.

what_good_looks_like: Every agent run emits machine-readable evidence: prompt/profile, diff, commands, tests, docs checks, retrieved context, findings, skipped checks, and final disposition.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit prompts ask for evidence-backed findings, defines the CLI-later `codex-review trace` concept for local evidence maps, and repo-contract-kit installs receipt-aware task readiness/finalizer/self-heal/automation surfaces plus a read-only agent-state ledger that indexes checkout dirt, task metadata/worktrees, closeout state, sidecar receipts, finalizer/final receipts, automation baselines, local attribution, unresolved blockers, and next safe commands.
external_examples: OpenAI Euphony; SWE-bench/SWE-agent trajectory patterns; microsoft/agent-governance-toolkit evidence; Codex terminal/test evidence
ideas_to_borrow: Keep extending common receipt schemas and source prompt requirements so review, implementation, automation, attribution, trace, and closeout receipts stay comparable across tools.
gap_or_risk: Receipt coverage is local-first and now has a source trace concept
priority: but a shipped trace CLI still needs to preserve local-only provenance and redaction boundaries without leaking private thread context.

what_good_looks_like: Reviewer personas are versioned, validated, source-scoped, run in isolated workspaces when mutating, and synthesize into one severity-ranked report.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit has strong persona prompts and synthesis, but no persona manifest schema, validator, or automated dispatch runner.
external_examples: open-code-review; codex-subagents-mcp; Claude /ultrareview; SuperClaude; BMAD-METHOD
ideas_to_borrow: Add persona manifest with allowed actions, source boundaries, expected JSON schema, and max finding budget.
gap_or_risk: Prompts alone can duplicate work, create noisy findings, or blur read-only versus write responsibilities.

what_good_looks_like: Code-changing work starts from a failing test or characterization test where feasible, records red/green evidence, and treats generated tests as reviewable artifacts.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit has a TDD prompt set, and repo-contract-kit strict receipt validation now recognizes behavior-changing scope and requires red/green evidence or an explicit skip reason.
external_examples: TDD Guard; Agent Teams TDD; SWE-bench; ChatGPT unit test generation studies; Simon Willison X discussion
ideas_to_borrow: Keep generated-test provenance visible and add future PR-readiness integration so receipt evidence feeds merge decisions.
gap_or_risk: Prompt-level TDD can still degrade if agents skip the receipt gate or mark behavior changes as docs-only.

what_good_looks_like: Source changes are localized to affected docs/contracts, deterministic checks gate missing docs, executable references stay fresh, AI can explain or propose docs patches, and waivers are structured.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit has docs-impact checks, waiver semantics, docs-freshness checks for local links/Make targets/script refs/schema refs, optional semantic receipt enforcement, docs-propose, docs-explain, and an explicit experimental docs-as-tests profile for manifest-declared local JSON OpenAPI method/path assertions. agent-workflow-kit has doc-code-delta persona, learning-comments mode, and advisory comment/docstring drift labels with two-sided evidence and false-positive handling.
external_examples: ReadMe GitHub AI Writer; RepoAgent; CASCADE; DocChecker; docs-as-code HN thread; Codacy AI summaries
ideas_to_borrow: Add API-change detection, richer docs patch proposal flows, explicit task-packet docs-surface requirements, and source-checkout checks that keep task-packet research handoffs plus documented Make targets aligned.
gap_or_risk: Docs checks now cover local links/commands/schema refs, one explicit API docs-as-tests assertion type, and advisory comment/docstring drift labels, but broader claim manifests, confidence/safety tiers, executable snippet coverage, richer OpenAPI semantics, packet-to-summary coverage, and dogfood Make target drift remain follow-ons.

what_good_looks_like: Local CLI, GitHub Action, and PR comment commands expose docs-impact, review-docs, explain-waiver, add-docs, update-changelog, and verify modes.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit has Makefile targets, CI templates, a docs-contract PR comment workflow, spec-only slash-command grammar, local read-only docs-explain Q&A, and changelog-update proposal/check helpers; agent-workflow-kit has prompts but no hosted PR bot interface.
external_examples: PR-Agent/Qodo; CodeRabbit; Continue PR Review Bot; GitHub Copilot; Greptile; Devin Review
ideas_to_borrow: Keep local read/proposal/check commands as the stable base, then add hosted slash-command execution only after structured outputs and permission boundaries stay reliable.
gap_or_risk: Hosted or autonomous comments can create noise before finding quality

feature_area: Safe outputs and CI control plane
what_good_looks_like: Agent jobs run read-only on untrusted PRs, emit safe JSON outputs, use allowlisted tools/network, and require human approval before write-back or merge.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit has profiles but not a compiled/locked agentic workflow with safe-output schemas.
external_examples: GitHub Agentic Workflows; microsoft/agent-governance-toolkit; Mergify; SonarQube AI Code Assurance; agentic CI X discussions
ideas_to_borrow: Define safe output schemas and strict CI mode before enabling write-back workflows.
gap_or_risk: Agentic CI without trust boundaries can become a repo mutation and security risk.

what_good_looks_like: Reviewers retrieve definitions, usages, tests, docs, ADRs, runtime configs, and ownership context before making findings.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit now has `scripts/build_context_packet.py` / `make context-packet` for deterministic changed-file context, `workflows/prompts/templates/review-map.md` plus `schemas/review-map.schema.json` for large-changeset navigation, and an AGW-034 spike recommending a later optional local-only LSP artifact for definitions/usages when language servers are already available.
external_examples: Sourcebot; mrge.io; AutoCodeRover; Agentless; CodeRabbit codegraph; Devin DeepWiki
ideas_to_borrow: Keep deterministic context packets and review maps as the required fallback, then add LSP-backed lookup only as a bounded local artifact with no auto-install, no remote indexing, hard timeouts, token caps, generated/vendor exclusions, and explicit omissions.
gap_or_risk: The remaining risk is implementation discipline: LSP lookup can improve symbol grounding, but missing servers, monorepo ambiguity, dynamic language gaps, cache invalidation, and noisy references must stay best-effort and artifact-only until fixtures prove value.

what_good_looks_like: Reports cap findings, require severity/confidence/evidence, suppress nits by default, and track accepted/rejected/fixed outcomes.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit prompts reject weak findings, but no metrics or outcome tracking exists.
external_examples: AI code review bubble HN discussion; Code Review Automation TSE; CodeRabbit feedback memory; Cursor learned rules
ideas_to_borrow: Add finding budgets, outcome tracking, rule-hit analytics, and accepted-finding-to-policy workflow.
gap_or_risk: AI review can add burden if it optimizes coverage instead of reviewer attention.

what_good_looks_like: Write-capable agents run in separate worktrees/branches/sandboxes; read-only reviewers cannot mutate files; auto-merge is off by default.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit documents the worktree-per-task pattern, and repo-contract-kit installs prepare/status, explicit dirty-primary baseline capture for task preparation, guarded self-heal for sidecar initialization and stale generated task-state quarantine, a read-only state ledger, a local task-readiness gate with baseline drift blocking, lifecycle/finalizer commands with locking, run ids, owner/session/thread/automation attribution, latest receipt provenance, heartbeats, leases, linked receipts, active-task context packets, nested-worktree cleanup audits, and evidence-gated closeout with retention for clean terminal sibling worktrees.
external_examples: open-swe; Optio; Cline checkpoints; Devin isolated worktree; GitHub Copilot ephemeral Actions environment; Git worktree remove; Codex app managed worktree cleanup
ideas_to_borrow: Keep improving merge handoff and richer scope/actual-diff overlap detection now that task-worktree readiness, dirty-primary baselines, guarded self-heal, state ledger, attribution, and finished-worktree closeout have guarded local commands.
gap_or_risk: Parallel agents can still conflict at integration time if merge readiness is inferred from partial CI or hosted branch-protection state instead of the local evidence gate plus later PR governance.
source_types: github; hacker_news; google_search; x; official_docs; local_repo

what_good_looks_like: One canonical prompt/skill definition exports to Codex skills, Claude commands/subagents, Cursor rules, Continue rules, Copilot instructions, and plain Markdown.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit now records generated and planned adapters in `workflows/manifest.json`; Codex and GitHub Copilot projections are generated from the shared source; docs/runtime-compatibility.md distinguishes generated, planned, manual-only, and unsupported/unknown runtime surfaces; repo-contract-kit owns target install/update selection. Post-consolidation docs still need legacy/archive wording where they mention this checkout as source truth, and AGW-127 should migrate the surviving workflow-source artifacts into the v1 repo instead of maintaining two source histories.
external_examples: speckit-agent-skills; LambdaTest agent-skills; SuperClaude; Packmind; Claude ecosystem prompt packs
ideas_to_borrow: Keep the boundary explicit during migration: source-side generation belongs in the v1 repository's workflows tree and target-repo selection or updates belong to the same `kit` CLI; add future runtime projections only with primary-source docs
gap_or_risk: an owner
primary_repo: Additional agent runtimes remain manual-only until source generation
source_types: and validation commands exist; copied runtime-specific prompt files can still drift without those owners or clear legacy labels. The larger risk is preserving old workflow-source history without leaving a second normal edit path.

what_good_looks_like: Learning mode verifies docs, latest ADRs, existing comments, and actual code; adds only intent/invariant/context comments; stale comments are flagged or fixed.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit has a dedicated learning-comments prompt, deterministic latest-ADR/no-ADR fallback guidance, strict comment-only receipt evidence, non-developer explanation-note guidance, and doc-code-delta advisory comment/docstring drift labels. repo-contract-kit has the learning preset and installed strict receipt validation for learning-comments. No automated stale-comment scanner exists by design.
external_examples: DocChecker; Claude Code project memory patterns; Reddit CLAUDE.md/rules patterns; RepoAgent
ideas_to_borrow: Keep comment-only receipt proof and fallback-evidence discipline strong; add any automated stale-comment scanner only after advisory labels and false-positive data justify it.
gap_or_risk: Comments can become trusted documentation and therefore need drift checks too
priority: but scanner-style enforcement can create noise without calibrated false-positive data.

what_good_looks_like: Local fixture suite measures docs-impact detection, review false positives, test-first compliance, instruction freshness, and human review burden over time.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit now has docs-impact benchmark fixtures, prompt regression fixtures, and optional session receipt outcome/effort metrics for review yield, false-positive and duplicate rates, latency, time-to-green, token/cost effort, and human burden; both repos have tests for implemented scripts.
external_examples: SWE-bench; SWE-agent; SWE-CI; Agentless; LLM4SE survey; Reddit paper-summary caution
ideas_to_borrow: Extend synthetic and real-history tasks so receipt checks calibrate outcome and burden metrics against expected review quality.
gap_or_risk: Without broader eval history, prompt changes can still feel better while reducing actual signal.

what_good_looks_like: Users can run review and docs-governance checks locally with clear data boundaries, optional local models, and no account-state mutations.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit now has local/private review policy, read-only browser research policy, and review-only local-model suitability guidance with data-boundary labels, capability caveats, escalation triggers, and source-cited runtime compatibility notes. It does not install models, add generated adapters, or claim local models are universally private/correct/productive.
external_examples: OpenClaw private browser agent; Goose; Aider; Open Codex; Continue runner-local processing; Greptile self-hosting
ideas_to_borrow: Keep local/private guidance caveated and source-cited as runtimes change; only add generated adapters after primary docs, install ownership, validation commands, and data-boundary receipts exist.
gap_or_risk: Research and review agents can still leak private code/context or over-trust weak local-model findings if receipts omit endpoint boundaries

what_good_looks_like: A deterministic branch-or-PR readiness surface aggregates local evidence and CI/check inputs, while hosted branch-protection and merge-queue rules remain optional adapters over that local contract.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit now has branch-readiness JSON plus docs-only examples that map local evidence to GitHub protected branches required status checks and merge queue handoff without giving the kit hosted mutation authority.
external_examples: GitHub protected branches docs; GitHub status checks docs; GitHub merge queue docs; projscan evidence-pack
ideas_to_borrow: Keep hosted merge governance separate from local task-worktree readiness and require explicit owner/platform action for PR or queue state changes.
gap_or_risk: Agents may loop polling CI or mistake partial green checks or branch-protection UI state for true merge readiness.

what_good_looks_like: Backlog items are agent-ready packets with scope, acceptance criteria, verification, docs impact, risk, coordination context, metric hints, goal alignment, previous-task closeout state, owner/approval state, and optional bounded phase files for large work.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit has a task-packet prompt/template/schema with coordination, harness metric, closeout, goal-alignment, previous_task_state, closeout_required_before_start, and optional phase_files fields, plus safe-start/refuse-start/blocker-escalation and phase-scope prompt language; repo-contract-kit discovers CSV/Markdown backlog sources, validates source/mirror health, exposes backlog-status/backlog-check/agent-next, scaffolds task packets from selected ids, maps changed files through installed area contracts, preserves recurring backlog/research edits as sidecar patches and receipts without dirtying live checkouts, and installs docs-only planning adapter examples for Keryx, Obsidian, issue trackers, spreadsheets, and repo backlog mirrors.
external_examples: Factory Droids; open-swe; Sweep; Reddit plan/phase workflows; GitHub Spec Kit tasks
ideas_to_borrow: Keep prioritization in Keryx, issues, CSV, or repo mirrors while improving handoff examples only when they stay docs-only and local-first; require implementation packets to name exact docs and release metadata surfaces when public contracts change.
gap_or_risk: Backlog commands report source health, preserve automation handoffs, and attach area-goal signals, but they still do not replace human priority decisions or external planning-system authority. Task packets can still be too vague about which docs satisfy public CLI/API/config/schema changes.

what_good_looks_like: The workflow stack is treated as an explicit agent harness: environment setup, context selection, permission policy, task isolation, receipts, eval fixtures, source reports, and verification gates are mapped, measured, and improved as harness components.
our_current_state: The repos have mapped harness primitives in docs/harness-engineering.md, task-packet and receipt schemas carry coordination plus harness_metrics fields, repo-contract-kit emits installed backlog/lifecycle/docs/token/goal-check surfaces with tests, and recurring research loops now carry novelty ledgers plus candidate scoring.
external_examples: OpenAI Evals; SWE-agent; OpenHands; LangGraph; Microsoft AutoGen; AGW-007; AGW-032; AGW-039; AGW-056; AGW-062
ideas_to_borrow: Extend regression fixtures for scope drift, missing context, incomplete receipts, permission mismatch, integration readiness, goal-alignment drift, and repeated low-novelty research loops.
gap_or_risk: The next risk is ensuring fixture coverage catches regressions in goal-check routing and novelty gating as prompt adapters and recurring research loops evolve.

what_good_looks_like: Maintainer control-plane prompts classify repository work into Active, Needs owner, Ready next, and Blocked without taking mutation, release, or credential permission for granted.
our_current_state: agent-workflow-kit now has a maintainer queue prompt that assembles repo-contract-kit signals from backlog-status/agent-next, task packets, task status, lifecycle metadata, readiness receipts, and automation handoff into a local-first owner-decision report.
external_examples: maintainer-orchestrator skill; AGW-054; AGW-061; AGW-066; AGW-070; AGW-082
ideas_to_borrow: Keep queue orchestration as prompt-level source work unless a separate target-repo command is justified; any future installed status API belongs in repo-contract-kit.
gap_or_risk: A prompt can standardize the operator report, but it still depends on existing local commands and explicit authority for mutation, release, credentials, or thread management.

what_good_looks_like: Prompts, context packets, source reports, receipts, adapters, and installed bundles carry explicit token budgets and compression rules while preserving required evidence, safety boundaries, and docs-contract signals.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit now installs check_token_budget.py, make agent-token-budget, and make agent-context-bundle so agents can consume bounded dirty-state, backlog, task-status, docs-impact, goal-check, token-budget, sidecar, and readiness-hint reports with explicit omissions; agent-workflow-kit 0.2.17 teaches task-packet, maintainer-queue, verification, and prompt-index guidance to consume compact deterministic reports before broad repo rereads while preserving scoped source fallback and omission evidence.
external_examples: OpenAI prompt caching docs; Anthropic prompt caching and context editing docs; prompt compression/context engineering papers; AGW-032; AGW-041; AGW-062
ideas_to_borrow: Promote token-budget reports to strict repo-specific budgets once warning data is stable and add more consumers that fail early on missing, stale, blocked, or ambiguous compact reports.
gap_or_risk: Prompt and artifact growth can still increase cost and latency if compact report consumers are optional or strict budgets are not tuned.

feature_area: Kit CLI interactivity and agent introspection
what_good_looks_like: The public `kit` command is scenario-driven for humans, structured for agents, non-interactive by contract when scripted, and backed by generated command metadata, completions, actionable errors, compact summaries, optional styling, local feedback capture, and regression fixtures.
our_current_state: repo-contract-kit 0.6.20 has a structured command catalogue with route taxonomy fields, recoverable parse-error envelopes, scenario-grouped help/options, status text that separates version roles, an explicit `--no-input` / `KIT_AGENT=1` non-interactive contract with JSON mode metadata, compact human summaries for update and doctor flows, command-map `json_contract` schema pointers, stable field metadata, explicit non-JSON reasons, `version --json` no-write metadata, `kit completion bash|zsh|fish` generated from parser and command-map metadata, TTY-only `kit palette` command-map-backed search with exact command previews and mutating-command confirmation, generated `kit cli-reference` docs with freshness gating, fixture-backed CLI UX regression checks plus review guidance for intentional wording changes, optional stdlib-only `--style auto|plain|pretty` summary emphasis that respects `NO_COLOR` while keeping JSON unstyled, `kit feedback` as a local sidecar JSONL ledger with no network calls, no upstream submission, and no target-repo writes, `kit agent-tool-manifest --json` as a command-map-derived local agent manifest with safe commands, write-command groups, schemas, examples, no-input behavior, and no hosted/network/credential/write behavior, and `kit_drift` diagnostics on status/doctor surfaces that compare global-tool and target-install version/source-ref/prompt-snapshot metadata, classify acceptable, stale, newer-target, unknown, or not-installed states, and print safe dry-run/update/global-update commands. AGW-127 should make `kit` the single CLI for the v1 repository rather than a launcher over historically separate source/install repos. Remaining gaps are task-start freshness policy and v1 consolidation.
external_examples: CLIG; GitHub CLI manual; HN CLI UX threads; Cloudflare schema-driven CLI; Tilebox agent-context; Charm Gum; prompt_toolkit
ideas_to_borrow: Build a command-map-first roadmap under one v1 repo: structured discovery, better errors, grouped help/status, explicit no-input/agent mode, summary renderers, alias taxonomy, JSON/schema consistency, completions, command palette, generated docs, UX fixtures, optional styling, feedback ledger, agent tool manifest, drift diagnostics, and startup freshness checks that default to report/dry-run rather than unconditional mutation.
gap_or_risk: The command contract, JSON/schema consistency, generated completions, generated CLI reference docs, CLI UX fixtures, TTY command palette, optional summary styling, local feedback ledger, agent manifest, kit drift diagnostics, recoverable parse errors, scenario-first help, non-interactive mode, compact update/doctor summaries, and canonical/alias/agent/maintainer route taxonomy now exist, but later improvements still need to consume the command map consistently across daily summaries, v1 consolidation diagnostics, and task-start update policy.
